title: rupicapnos africana var. mauritanica
intro: Rupicapnos africana var. mauritanica, also known as the yellow horned poppy or horned poppy, is a variety of the Rupicapnos africana plant, a flowering plant native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to induce sedation, relieve anxiety and insomnia, and act as a mild analgesic. The health benefits of Rupicapnos africana var. mauritanica include its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant often include extracts in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
